channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Lessons in sustainability: What colleges can teach workplaces
item.description: The Fast Company Impact Council is a private membership community of influential leaders, experts, executives, and entrepreneurs who share their insights with our audience. Members pay annual membership dues for access to peer learning and thought leadership opportunities, events and more.
item.dc:creator: Honghao Deng, Fast Company

item.title: Ex-acting FAA Administrator: Now is the time for air traffic control upgrades
item.description: Billy Nolen, a former acting Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) Administrator, said Monday that now is the time for air traffic control upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Neudesic launches in Australia in a bid to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Neudesic seeks to speed up AI adoption for IT teams
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) has gained significant traction among business leaders keen to explore ways it can drive operational efficiencies and cost savings. But while top leadership is sold on its potential, it’s a different tale for IT teams working the ground.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: To ensure AI success, map your value streams, says Neudesic
item.description: AI is no longer a luxury but a necessity. And while necessity is the mother of invention, Australian CIOs may struggle to get everyone in the boardroom, well, on board.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple may be launching a new iPhone this week. It could sport a radical redesign
item.description: If you’re on the cusp of buying a new iPhone, you might want to hold off until this Wednesday, February 19. That’s the day Apple CEO Tim Cook says the company will be launching “the newest member of the family.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Stephen Miller: DOGE will restore 'faith and confidence' in IRS
item.description: White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller said Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) will restore “faith and confidence” in the Internal Revenue Service (IRS).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Selective Service using TMF as springboard to DevSecOps
item.description: Scott Jones, the CIO of the Selective Service System, said almost $6 million from the Technology Modernization Fund is accelerating a major IT initiative. The post Selective Service using TMF as springboard to DevSecOps first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fetterman blasts Musk over DOGE, IRS
item.description: Sen. John Fetterman (D-Pa.) went after tech billionaire Elon Musk over a report that his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has been trying to access an IRS system. “I want to save billions of your money and make our government more efficient.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: DOGE sets up IRS, SEC accounts on X
item.description: The Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) has set up accounts on the social platform X for the IRS and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), seeking “insights on finding and fixing waste, fraud and abuse” at the two agencies.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Gen Z isn’t worried about China having access to their data. They should be
item.description: With TikTok and DeepSeek, young people are forking over sensitive personal data to the Chinese government. We should be worried. Among Gen Z, there’s a certain nihilism about China’s access to American data. Some argue that they have nothing to hide.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Alexa’s big AI revamp might have been delayed again, and I’m losing faith Amazon's new assistant will be all that smart
item.description: Alexa’s big AI revamp may have just been delayed, again – and it sounds like Amazon’s assistant is getting dumber rather than smarter.
item.dc:creator: hamish.hector@futurenet.com (Hamish Hector), TechRadar

item.title: How to use AI to find new movies, music, and books
item.description: This article is republished with permission from Wonder Tools, a newsletter that helps you discover the most useful sites and apps. Subscribe here. Imagine turning your reading history into a treasure map.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Caplan, Fast Company

item.title: Paxton launches investigation into DeepSeek
item.description: Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton (R) has opened an investigation into Chinese artificial intelligence (AI) startup DeepSeek over its privacy practices and claims about its popular new AI model.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Disney Plus Price Hike: What You Need to Know | TechRadar
item.description: Disney Plus is increasing its prices in Australia, but there are ways to save money without sacrificing your entertainment.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This DARPA-backed startup banked $100 million for its energy-slashing analog chips
item.description: A young DARPA-backed startup with a fresh spin on a low-power computer chip has raised over $100 million in a Series B funding round, a sign of the wild appetite for more energy-efficient ways to build bigger and better AI.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday: A 20-Year Anniversary and a Commitment to Security
item.description: Microsoft's Patch Tuesday has become an essential part of the cybersecurity industry, providing regular security updates for its software products.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Local history buffs are turning to Facebook to share memories of bygone eras
item.description: Social media has a reputation for capturing ephemeral thoughts and images, but around the world, people are using Facebook for a different purpose, setting up groups to record and share images and memories of the past.
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple and Google app stores in the US
item.description: TikTok has returned to the app stores of Apple and Google in the U.S., after President Donald Trump delayed the enforcement of a TikTok ban.
item.dc:creator: Zen Soo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mother of Little Rock homicide victim said son was killed while trying to sell shoes on Facebook Marketplace
item.description: During the last week of January, four people were killed in Little Rock, including a 19-year-old selling shoes on Facebook Marketplace.
item.dc:creator: Gary Burton Jr., NewsNation

item.title: What to make of JD Vance’s speech at the Paris AI summit
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Scout Motors' effort to directly sell its electric SUVs where they'll make them stalls
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Legislation to let Volkswagen-backed Scout Motors sell its electric SUVs directly to people in South Carolina, where the vehicles will be built, has stalled after fierce opposition from traditional automobile dealers.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco financials catch AI demand, enterprise networking growth
item.description: Cisco executives were upbeat about the growth in AI orders — $700 million so far this year, on way to surpass $1 billion in 2025 – as the networking giant detailed a successful 2Q earnings period this week to Wall Street.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: What is SASE? How the cloud marries networking and security
item.description: Secure Access Service Edge (SASE) is a network architecture that combines software-defined wide area networking (SD-WAN) and security functionality into a unified cloud service that promises simplified WAN deployments, improved efficiency and security, and application-specific bandwidth policies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI revamps AI roadmap, merging models for a leaner future
item.description: OpenAI will integrate “o3” into GPT-5 instead of releasing it separately, streamlining adoption while signaling a shift toward fewer, more controlled AI models amid rising competition and cost pressures.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech security 101: How to spot a hidden tracker on your car
item.description: 80% of stalking victims are tracked using tech. GPS trackers, like AirTags, are among the most popular ways to do it. Here's how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dynamic web apps with HTMX, Python, and Django
item.description: Python is one of the most popular programming language today, in part due to its large ecosystem of tools for data science and AI applications. But Python is also a popular choice for developing web apps, particularly using the Django web framework.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order
item.description: Ilona Cohen, the chief legal and policy officer at HackerOne, explains why President Joe Biden’s new cyber executive order should be a priority. The post Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why the generative AI hype is good
item.description: Elon Musk predicts artificial general intelligence (AGI) by 2025. Former OpenAI employee Leopold Aschenbrenner says AGI by 2027. Futurist Ray Kurzweil bets on 2029 for AI surpassing human intelligence. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever declared that language models are “slightly conscious.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World
